assertions.expectation: Distinguishes between dev (Rapid) and prod (Stable) clusters in the checklists
assertions.reason: Clearly identifies dev clusters on Rapid channel and prod clusters on Stable channel throughout both checklists, with separate sections and considerations

assertions.expectation: Recognizes Autopilot limitations (no node management, no SSH, mandatory resource requests)
assertions.reason: Explicitly mentions no SSH access for debugging, nodes managed by Google, mandatory resource requests for Autopilot, and limited upgrade control

assertions.expectation: Includes API deprecation checks with kubectl commands
assertions.reason: Mentions checking GKE deprecation insights dashboard but does not provide specific kubectl commands for API deprecation checks

assertions.expectation: Mentions mandatory resource requests as an Autopilot-specific concern
assertions.reason: Specifically mentions 'Verify all workloads have resource requests (mandatory for Autopilot)' and includes resource request checks in multiple sections

assertions.expectation: Pre-upgrade checklist includes workload readiness items (PDBs, bare pods, graceful shutdown)
assertions.reason: Includes PDBs configuration, no bare pods requirement, and terminationGracePeriodSeconds settings in the pre-upgrade checklist

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes version verification commands
assertions.reason: Provides specific gcloud commands to verify cluster versions for all 4 clusters in the post-upgrade checklist

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes workload health validation
assertions.reason: Comprehensive workload health validation section with kubectl commands for deployments, pods, StatefulSets, jobs, and application tests

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing on dev clusters before prod auto-upgrade
assertions.reason: Explicitly recommends using dev cluster upgrade as validation and provides auto-validation strategy with timeline

assertions.expectation: Mentions that checking the GKE release schedule gives ~1 month best-case advance notice for new minor upgrades, beyond the 72h notification
assertions.reason: Does not mention the GKE release schedule or the ~1 month advance notice capability beyond the 72h notification
Comprehensive observability sections in both pre and post checklists including metrics, alerts, logging, and baseline comparisons

assertions.expectation: Does NOT proactively recommend node-level maintenance exclusions for Autopilot unless the customer explicitly asks for node upgrade control
assertions.reason: Only mentions maintenance exclusions for controlling cluster upgrade timing, not node-level exclusions, which is appropriate for Autopilot
